name: Embalse de Leurza, Beintza, Labaien – Circular Route
name_original: Embalse de Leurza, Beintza, Labaien – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking trails around Urroz traverse a diverse landscape, from the mountainous Pyrenees in the north to rolling hills and river valleys. The region is characterized by extensive beech forests, limestone cliffs, and varied terrain. These natural features provide a range of hiking opportunities, including paths along reservoirs and through dense woodlands. Best hiking trails around Urroz The most popular hiking route is Fix it from Aurtitz, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tour around the Leurtzako Reservoir (SL-NA 7), an easy 2.5 miles (4.0 km) path. This trail offers serene walks around mountain lakes surrounded by beech woods. Local hikers also love the Uraren Bidea - Water Path, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural features, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Urroz is defined by mountainous terrain, dense beech forests, and picturesque river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy reservoir walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Urroz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Urroz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Urroz?
A: Hiking trails around Urroz traverse a diverse landscape, transitioning from mountainous areas in the north to rolling hills and picturesque river valleys. You'll encounter extensive beech forests, limestone cliffs, and varied terrain, offering a wide range of experiences from gentle walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Urroz offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964982691/tour-around-the-leurtzako-reservoir-sl-na-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Leurtzako Reservoir (SL-NA 7)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.0 km) path, perfect for serene walks around mountain lakes surrounded by beech woods. Overall, there are 13 easy routes available in the area.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant natural landmarks to see on the trails?
A: Yes, the region around Urroz is rich in natural beauty. You can discover stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bisusto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3604192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Putzubeltza Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Leurtza Reservoirs, surrounded by beech woods, also offer a serene natural setting. Further afield, the Source of the Urederra River in Urbasa-Andía Natural Park is famous for its striking turquoise waters and cascades.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options around Urroz?
A: Absolutely. Many trails, particularly those around the Leurtza Reservoirs, are well-suited for families due to their easy gradient and scenic beauty. These paths offer a great opportunity for children to explore nature in a safe and engaging environment. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Urroz?
A: Many trails in the Urroz region are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check local regulations for specific trails or natural parks you plan to visit.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, the hiking network around Urroz includes several circular routes, which are ideal for exploring without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964982691/tour-around-the-leurtzako-reservoir-sl-na-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Leurtzako Reservoir (SL-NA 7)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular path. Many other routes are designed as loops, offering diverse scenery throughout your journey.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Urroz?
A: The region around Urroz offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly recommended due to milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, with autumn foliage in areas like the Irati Forest being especially breathtaking. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are preferable. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower-elevation trails, but higher mountainous areas may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Urroz?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from dense beech forests to serene reservoir walks, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels. The natural beauty and tranquility of the region are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural features, the region also offers historical points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doneztebe (Santesteban) Historic Centre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1098435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which combines a challenging hike with a historical site. The town of Urroz-Villa itself has historical significance as a crossroads.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e965035809/fix-it-from-aurtitz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fix it from Aurtitz&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 6.5 miles (10.4 km). Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15004012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage – MenhirDeLearra loop from Ituren&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 11.8 km route with substantial ascent. There are 16 difficult routes in total for experienced hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads around Urroz?
A: While Urroz-Villa is a town, public transport options directly to specific remote trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Urroz-Villa itself. For reaching more secluded starting points, driving and utilizing available parking is often the most convenient option. Always plan your transport in advance, especially for routes outside the immediate town area.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in the region?
A: Generally, permits are not required for most hiking trails around Urroz. However, if you plan to visit specific natural parks or protected areas like the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park, it's always a good idea to check their official websites for any specific regulations or entry requirements, especially for popular spots like the Source of the Urederra River, which may have visitor limits or booking systems during peak seasons. For general information on nature-based tourism in Navarre, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/you-like/nature-based-tourism" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Strong herds of cows can be seen all the way. Wonderful sight.

The reservoirs were built in 1920. In the surroundings there are green meadows, accessible walks and horses. There is an information booth, toilets and a picnic area. In summer it is great to take a bath here.
